items.title: email_logs migration applies and rolls back cleanly
items.precondition: Postgres reachable via the app connection
items.expected: Table created with 18 columns (jsonb recipients/cc/bcc/original_recipients, timestamptz timestamps) + 4 indexes; down() drops it; re-apply clean.
items.evidence: Batch 129 run/rolled-back/run on local PG; verified 18 cols + email_logs_{category,status,created_at,provider_message_id}_idx; hasTable=false after rollback.
items.notes: Local (localhost/appdb) validation; dev DB migration runs on deploy.

items.title: EmailLogModel round-trips against Postgres
items.expected: Insert stores jsonb arrays; list omits body_html and filters correctly; detail returns body_html; delivered sets status+delivered_at (1 row); opened advances to opened+opened_at (1 row).
items.evidence: Round-trip script: recipients came back as parsed array, body_html absent in list / present in detail, delivered→opened correlation both updated 1 row.

items.title: sendEmail choke-point logs every send, non-blocking
steps: 2. confirm recordEmailLog wrapped in try/catch that never throws
items.expected: Both branches call recordEmailLog; a logging error is swallowed (WARN) and never fails/blocks the send; provider_message_id stored bracket-normalized.
items.evidence: tsc 0; recordEmailLog internal try/catch; success + failure branches both record. Behavioral send verification is T10.

items.title: Category set at every live sender
items.expected: Each live sender passes an explicit category; unset paths default to uncategorized.
items.evidence: Tagged: AuthPasswordController x3 (auth), user-management x2 + AdminUserPasswordService (account), billingEmails x2 (billing), SupportEmailService x3 (support), sendToAdmins + UserSendNotification (notification), leadgen wrapper (leadgen), website wrapper (website_form), worker-watchdog (system). tsc 0.
items.notes: Dead dist-only checkup templates left untagged.

items.title: Signed Mailgun event webhook flips rows (delivered/opened)
items.precondition: Deployed to dev; MAILGUN_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY set; Mailgun event webhook + open-tracking configured; a logged email with a provider_message_id
steps: 1. Send a Mailgun test event (or real) to POST /api/webhooks/mailgun-events
2. Send an event with a bad signature
items.expected: Valid delivered event flips the matching row to delivered (+delivered_at); bad signature → 401; duplicate event is idempotent; missing key → 503 (dormant, boot unaffected).
items.notes: Blocked on ops: signing key + Mailgun dashboard config + n8n Respond node returning the Mailgun id. Code + signature verify unit-shaped and build-clean.

items.title: Admin list + detail API is super-admin gated
items.precondition: Deployed to dev; a @getalloro.com admin JWT; at least one email_logs row
steps: 3. call both without/with a non-admin token
items.expected: Authed internal admin gets the §8.1 { success, data } envelope (paginated list; detail with body_html); non-admin → 403; bad id → 400; missing → 404.
items.notes: Build-clean; behavioral run needs the dev deploy.

items.title: Email Logs dashboard renders, filters, and previews HTML safely
items.precondition: Deployed to dev; logged in as @getalloro.com admin at dev.getalloro.com/admin
steps: 1. Open the Email Logs sidebar item
2. Filter by category/status/date, search a recipient
4. Confirm the body renders in a sandboxed iframe (no script execution)
items.expected: List loads with category/status badges (opened marked best-effort *), filters + pagination work, detail modal shows metadata + the exact sent HTML inside a sandbox='' iframe; intercepted rows show the original recipients.
items.notes: Frontend tsc + vite build pass; behavioral browser run needs the dev deploy.

items.title: A real email sent on dev appears in the dashboard
steps: 1. Trigger any email on dev (e.g. forgot-password for a @getalloro.com account, or a support ticket)
2. Open Email Logs and find it
items.expected: The email appears with the correct category, status=sent, intercepted flag (→ dave@getalloro.com on dev), and the exact rendered HTML in the viewer.
items.notes: End-to-end proof of the choke-point capture. Needs the dev deploy.
